_publications._publicationTitle: The Relationship Between Heart Rate Variability, Psychological Flexibility, and Pain in Neurofibromatosis Type 1.

_contextSnippet: HRV was negatively correlated with psychological inflexibility (r = −0.50, P < 0.01) and pain interference (r = −0.34, P < 0.05)...pain interference shared a significant positive association with psychological inflexibility (r = 0.54, P < 0.01)
resourceName: PROMIS Pain Interference scales (adult and pediatric short forms)
details: Pain interference showed significant negative correlation with HF-HRV (r = −0.34, P < 0.05) and positive correlation with psychological inflexibility (r = 0.54, P < 0.01)
